question: What is the manufacturing cluster readiness - can Chinese OEM factories actually build global brands?
company: Alibaba / JD / NetEase / Xiaomi (Platform Brand Precedents)

findings.claim: Taobao Xinxuan (淘宝心选), Alibaba's self-operated lifestyle brand launched in 2017, was discontinued by 2022 and merged into 'Miaomanfen' (喵满分) under Tmall Supermarket - a clear failure of the platform self-operated brand model
findings.data: Taobao Xinxuan launched May 2017. In H1 2020, moved from Taobao business group to new local retail group; months later, head Zhang Di departed. In 2021, merged into Tmall Supermarket under B2C retail group. The official store announced suspension of operations and rebranding to 'Miaomanfen' (喵满分) under Tmall Supermarket, ending the Taobao Xinxuan brand. Alibaba then launched 'Tao Yanxuan' (淘严选) in March 2025 as a new attempt - a quality curation service built on Tao Factory (淘工厂), signaling Alibaba hasn't given up but acknowledges prior failure.

findings.claim: JD Jingzao (京东京造) remains active with 200M+ user base by 2025, entering 'phase 3' of growth, but has never disclosed standalone revenue - success is ambiguous
findings.data: By 2025, JD Jingzao entered its third growth phase (from '1 to 10') with 200M+ user base. Partnered with 70%+ of domestic industrial belts (Shandong textiles, Jiangsu silk, Guangdong 3C). In 2025, proposed '1223' business development plan. Recent products include AI plush toys, AI alarm clocks, and Au99.99 gold phone cases. Actively integrating JD's self-developed JoyAI (100x token usage growth YoY). However: specific standalone revenue figures have never been disclosed separately in JD's financials.

findings.claim: NetEase Yanxuan (网易严选) missed its 2018 RMB 20B GMV target, faces traffic anxiety, and struggles against Pinduoduo, Douyin, and traditional platforms - demonstrating difficulty of the curated brand model
findings.data: NetEase founder Ding Lei set a 2018 GMV target of RMB 20B for Yanxuan - missed it (Yanxuan + Kaola combined reached only RMB 19.2B). By 2025, Yanxuan faces 'traffic anxiety' as it competes not just with Alibaba/JD/PDD but with content e-commerce platforms (Douyin, Xiaohongshu). Q3 2024: Yanxuan Pro membership grew 56% YoY, and pet/home cleaning categories achieved double-digit sales growth, but 'breakthrough remains the key challenge heading into 2025.' The brand is caught between idealistic quality positioning and price-war reality.

findings.claim: Xiaomi ecosystem invested in 400 companies (2016-2020) with ~330 still active as of 2021 (book value RMB 57.9B), but successful companies tend to 'escape' Xiaomi to build own brands due to low margins
findings.data: Xiaomi invested in 400 ecosystem chain companies (2016-2020) using 'minority stake, no control' model. As of Q2 2021, 330+ ecosystem enterprises with book value RMB 57.9B. Key pattern: successful companies 'escape' Xiaomi - Huami's Xiaomi wearable revenue share dropped from 92.1% (2016) to 66.9% (2018), then Xiaomi wearable shipments dropped 45.3% while Huami own-brand shipments grew 88.9% by Q3 2021. GGV Capital's Hans Tung: 'Xiaomi helped companies scale volume, but with low margins - they inevitably needed to build own brands for higher profits.' Whether ecosystem companies can survive independently remains an 'unanswerable pain point.'

findings.claim: Alibaba's newest attempt 'Tao Yanxuan' (March 2025) already faces quality control concerns - one product labeled as premium cotton was found to contain only 4.8% cotton
findings.data: Tao Yanxuan launched March 2025 covering 16 major categories. However, quality control issues emerged immediately: one product labeled as premium cotton was found to contain only 4.8% cotton content. A source factory merchant noted: 'raw material price fluctuations, rising labor costs, and supply chain instability all increase production costs and compress profit margins' - making it difficult to maintain quality-price ratio.
